page: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and contains no dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7a.2xlarge demonstrates strong single-core performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU testing and Geekbench single-core scores. Text processing is a notable strength, ranking in the top 10% on Geekbench. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves strong text generation speeds with small 135M models, but exhibits weak performance for text generation on medium 7B models.

The instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints, owing to its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. However, the absence of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, and the lack of a GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel processing. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing pipelines, web serving, and lightweight software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU capabilities, with top-tier performance in text processing and strong results in single-threaded decompression. However, multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain average, and text generation on medium 7B LLM models is weak. Given its 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and lack of local storage, the c7a.2xlarge is a cost-effective option for single-threaded applications, text processing, and web serving workloads that do not require high-density memory or local disk space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for text processing and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for single-threaded and text processing workloads.

16.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier text processing benchmark performance
No local storage and no GPU accelerators included
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, web serving, and text processing
